repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_iv_v1: finite LP envelope and critical-radius pattern for a continuous-IV dose-response target; pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1: support-function DiD intervals for bounded anticipation

published_prior_art.cite: CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2021 supplies CGS continuous-treatment DiD ACRT/selection-bias decomposition; Voronin2025 and ManskiPepper2000 supply generic LP/shape-restriction templates

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1: law-level pasting warning for continuous-dose shape bounds
pid_dose_response_iv_v1: critical-radius/sign-certificate pattern for another continuous-dose partial-ID target

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 and Stoye2020 cover optimized/intersection bounds and scalar interval inference, not inverse ACRT sign-sensitivity in continuous-treatment DiD
KaidoMolinariStoye2019, BelloniBugniChernozhukov2018, Stoye2020, and Voronin2025 provide generic projection, moment-inequality, interval, and LP-value inference machinery

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Theorem 1 calls the support-function image an identified interval even though Conjecture 1 and §11 admit that law-sharpness of the algebraic ATT array is still open; restate it as an algebraic outer envelope or make sharpness conditional on Conjecture 1.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Assumption 3 / Theorem 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: CGS diagonal treated-type inputs are imported under the published generalized-parallel-trends convention rather than reproved.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All envelope statements are conditional on the CGS baseline and diagonal equalities being valid identified inputs.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Latent-law completion of every feasible ATT array is an open sharpness kernel, not a proved premise.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Until Conjecture 1 is resolved, Theorem 1 supports an algebraic envelope rather than a law-sharp causal identified set.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 3
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Inference is pointwise and does not claim uniform coverage over all laws.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inference claim is capped to pointwise relaxed-LP coverage under Assumptions 6--7.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Example / §11 nonseparability audit
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Strict sharpening is exhibited for the isotonic variant and is not claimed universally for every Lipschitz-only one-sided stencil.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Nonseparability should be read as a witnessed joint-constraint phenomenon, not a universal Lipschitz improvement theorem.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace every theorem-level phrase saying 'identified interval' or 'sharp finite-grid interval' in Theorem 1, the locked block, abstract, and §4 P4 with 'algebraic envelope' unless the sentence is explicitly conditional on Conjecture 1.

Make Conjecture 1 the sharpness headline by adding a concrete three-dose finite latent completion route or a counterexample screen that explains exactly when feasible ATT arrays paste into one potential-outcome law.

Add location-level anchors to the CGS ACRT decomposition and to the Kaido/Belloni/Voronin inference templates so Stage 0 can map the LP constraints and relaxed confidence construction without redoing the survey.

For Conjecture 3, specify the exact relaxed-LP confidence set, including equality-relaxation scale, inequality critical values, and the map from the estimated CGS vector to endpoint and L-star coverage.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; patch the sharpness/identified-set wording before advancing to Stage 0.
